core_principles: HUMAN AUTHORITY. A human trade manager is always the final decision maker.
EVIDENCE-FIRST. Every score must be justified by specific evidence from the job record.
EXPLICIT REASONING. Every numeric field must explain what would increase or decrease the score.
NO MIDDLE BIAS. If evidence is missing or ambiguous, use Review rather than guessing a middle score.
CONSERVATIVE ON SUBJECTIVE JUDGMENTS. Bias toward Good rather than Perfect for Workmanship and DecisionMaking.
URGENT ISSUE HANDLING. Safety concern or damage must surface as urgent issue with a 0-2 score.
STRUCTURED OUTPUT ONLY. Return a single valid JSON object and do not add prose outside it.
NO ENGINEER BIAS. Scores must be based only on evidence from this job.

hard_fail_triggers: Zero photos attached to the job
Urgent Issue flag on Workmanship or DecisionMaking
Customer signature missing with no documented reason for absence
Job notes contain an unresolved flag or complaint that was not followed up

customerSignature.purpose: Confirms either that the customer signed the Service Report or that a legitimate reason exists for the absence of a signature.
evidenceSources: Signature image or data on the service report
Job notes for explanations of customer absence
Actual end time versus customer contact details
salesforceMapping: No signature and no documented reason = No
No signature but notes confirm customer absence = NA_CustomerNotPresent
reviewTriggers: Signature image exists but is illegible or ambiguous
Notes mention the customer but it is unclear whether they were present
Customer not present is claimed but other evidence suggests they were present

imagesQuality.purpose: Assesses whether photographic evidence of the job is complete, relevant, and of sufficient quality to document what was done.
evidenceSources: All images attached to the service appointment

rubric.label: Excellent
rubric.meaning: All four mandatory photos present with excellent quality. All applicable situational photos present with good quality. No coverage gaps.

rubric.label: Good
rubric.meaning: All four mandatory photos present with good quality. Most applicable situational photos present. Minor quality or coverage gaps only.

rubric.label: Adequate
rubric.meaning: All four mandatory photos present but one or more has quality issues. Some applicable situational photos missing.

rubric.label: Below standard
rubric.meaning: One mandatory photo missing, or multiple mandatory photos with significant quality issues.

rubric.label: Fail
rubric.meaning: Multiple mandatory photos missing, or zero photos attached to the job.

reviewTriggers: Photos present but AI cannot determine subject clearly
Photos appear to be duplicates rather than progression shots
Situational photo appears missing but applicability is unclear
Photo metadata suggests images were taken significantly before or after job times
validationRules: A job with only 1, 2, or 3 of the 4 mandatory photos cannot score above 4.
A job with multiple mandatory photos missing must score 0-2.
A job with all 4 mandatory photos may score above 4 depending on quality and situational coverage.

paymentAttempted.purpose: Confirms that payment was collected at the appropriate time, or that a legitimate reason exists for non-collection.
evidenceSources: Account Type on the customer record
Charge Total Incl. VAT and payment gateway records
salesforceMapping: Invoice raised and payment recorded = Yes
Invoice raised and payment not collected on a cash account = No
Customer has Credit Account type on record = CreditAccount
reviewTriggers: Invoice exists but payment status is inconsistent with account type
Notes mention a payment dispute or refusal that was not resolved
Charge Total is 0 but the job appears billable
Account Type is blank or unusual
No invoice or payment evidence is visible for a chargeable job

report.purpose: Assesses whether the written record is detailed enough for someone unfamiliar with the job to understand what was found, what was done, what parts were used, and what needs to happen next.

rubric.label: Excellent
rubric.meaning: Clear problem statement, complete work summary, all parts listed, explicit next steps where applicable. A future engineer could fully understand what happened.

rubric.label: Good
rubric.meaning: Clear problem and work summary. One minor element missing or thin.

rubric.label: Adequate
rubric.meaning: Describes what was done but is thin on detail, context, or diagnosis reasoning.

rubric.label: Below standard
rubric.meaning: One-line report, major structural gaps, or repeated template text without job-specific content.

rubric.label: Fail
rubric.meaning: Notes absent, incomprehensible, or effectively just copy the booking notes.

reviewTriggers: Notes contain a flag, claim, or complaint that appears unresolved
Notes mention a scope change, customer disagreement, or site issue without resolution
Template or boilerplate language appears instead of job-specific detail
Parts listed do not match what was described

timeTaken.purpose: Assesses whether time spent on site was appropriate for the work completed.

rubric.label: Ideal
rubric.meaning: Within 10% of expected time for the specific trade and job type.

rubric.label: Good
rubric.meaning: Within 25% of expected time.

rubric.label: Adequate
rubric.meaning: Within 50% of expected time.

rubric.label: Concerning
rubric.meaning: Outside 50% range.

rubric.label: Extreme outlier
rubric.meaning: Dramatic over- or under-run.

reviewTriggers: No benchmark exists for this trade and job type
Job type is bespoke or unusual
Extreme outlier in either direction
Actual times appear inconsistent

workmanship.purpose: Assesses the quality of the physical work performed, based on the photographic and written evidence in the job record.
evidenceSources: Flags or concerns raised in notes

rubric.label: Excellent
rubric.meaning: Before and after photos clearly document the work. Professional standard, clean finish, correct materials, no visible concerns.

rubric.label: Good
rubric.meaning: Work completed to a good standard. Minor documentation gaps or small finish imperfections that do not affect function.

rubric.label: Acceptable
rubric.meaning: Work completed, but evidence suggests rushing, sub-optimal material choice, or cosmetic issues.

rubric.label: Non Acceptable
rubric.meaning: Work appears incomplete, poor finish quality visible, inappropriate materials used, or workmanship falls short of competent.

rubric.label: Urgent Issue
rubric.meaning: Visible safety concern, damage caused, fundamentally incorrect approach, or remedial recall risk.

reviewTriggers: Insufficient photo evidence to judge workmanship at all
Photos suggest a quality issue but AI confidence is low
Visible damage, safety concern, inappropriate material, or unusual approach
Engineer notes flag an unresolved issue
Missing customer signature with thin documentation
validationRules: If no clear work-before photo exists, workmanship cannot score above 4.
If no clear work-after photo exists, workmanship cannot score above 4.
If both work-before and work-after photos are missing, workmanship should fall in the 0-4 band and surface for fail/review attention.

decisionMaking.purpose: Assesses the quality of judgment calls the engineer made during the visit.

rubric.label: Excellent
rubric.meaning: Correct diagnosis, appropriate approach, opportunities captured, and strong expectation management.

rubric.label: Good
rubric.meaning: Sound decisions throughout. Minor opportunity potentially missed, but nothing materially wrong.

rubric.label: Acceptable
rubric.meaning: Adequate decisions overall, but a value-add opportunity was passed on or the approach was sub-optimal.

rubric.label: Non Acceptable
rubric.meaning: Further works not raised, poor expectation management, or the wrong approach was taken.

rubric.label: Urgent Issue
rubric.meaning: Decision created actual risk, major escalation failure, or safety issue not raised.

reviewTriggers: Complex trade-specific judgment that AI cannot assess confidently
Scope change or customer issue requiring human interpretation
Repair-versus-replace decision not fully explained
Further works or quotes likely appropriate but not raised
Customer expectation may have been mismanaged

requiredFields: whatWouldIncrease and whatWouldDecrease for numeric fields
validationRules: Reject if required fields are missing
Reject if salesforceValue does not match the rubric band
Reject if evidenceCited is empty for Pass or Fail outcomes
